item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
All fixed!

That snuck in during my last edit. Thanks for catching it!

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
I like the SCP, definitely an interesting idea! Gets an upvote from me. :)

Also I'm not sure whether this is intentional but in "3567.doc.02: Concerning SCP-3567-1-C" you use SCP-XXXX rather than SCP-3567 a few times.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's much better now, although this makes the final Moose email kind of frivolous?
Still upvoted :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for your comment!

I've trimmed down the conproc and the description slightly, and I've beefed up the narrative thread a bit to give the interviews more meaning. I also shaped up the closing statement; it's got a different feel now, but I think it delivers a little better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for your comment!

I agree with both of your points. I've trimmed down the conproc and the description slightly, and I've beefed up the narrative thread a bit to give the interviews more meaning.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree with mallard's comments above on the conprocs.

Otherwise, with better wording on the closing statement and containment procedures, this is a definite upvote. Also I like how his new name has SCP in it, nice touch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The concept is certainly interesting. However there were two things that prevented me from upvoting.

First, he containment procedures and description could be trimmed to convey the same information with fewer words. As it is now it feels a bit sluggish and verbose.

Second, the interviews don't really flow narratively and feel disconnected. Especially the first one doesn't feel like a story and more a semi-related data point. If there was some common feeling linking the ideas through this piece could really come together and become a more cohesive whole.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
My first SCP is alive! Thanks to my reviewers and critters:

MalyceGraves
Simartar
DoctorFullham
DoctorLeibowitz

And all of the excellent people in IRC that answered all of my questions. I hope you enjoy it!

"House" by Jason Pratt is licensed under CC BY 2.0
